title: agastache palmeri var. leonensis
intro: Agastache palmeri var. leonensis, also known as Leon's agastache, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the mint family and is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including sore throats, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. Agastache palmeri var. leonensis can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting digestive health.
